definition: The Alesina-Ardagna (2010) 'expansionary austerity' result does not survive narrative identification methodology (Guajardo et al. 2014). Under proper identification, fiscal consolidations are contractionary with multipliers of 1.0-1.5x on average. Success conditions (200bp+ monetary easing, 10%+ exchange rate depreciation, gradualism) are largely unavailable to the U.S. in 2026.
evidence: Guajardo et al. 2014 narrative identification approach
Average GDP impact of 1% GDP consolidation: -0.62% (95% CI: -0.97% to -0.27%)
Only 15-20% of episodes show positive growth under narrative identification
3 of 4 successful large-scale consolidations required 15-25% currency depreciation
U.S. reserve currency status makes comparable depreciation politically/economically infeasible
open_questions: Can technology-driven efficiency gains (DOGE model) produce different outcomes than traditional spending cuts?
What fraction of government spending has near-zero multipliers and could be cut without demand impact?
